# Who am I?

My name is Santiago Zúñiga, born and raised in Argentina. I am a Mechanical Engineer with a Ph.D. focused on Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD). My work revolves around understanding complex fluid behaviors, exploring everything from multi-scale multiphase flows and thermal analysis to fluid-structure interactions. I regularly work with high-performance computing clusters to run DNS, LES and RANS simulations.

Beyond the computer screen, I also enjoy hands-on experimental work. I have practical experience in the lab conducting wind tunnel tests, hot-wire anemometry, and PIV measurements. Additionally, I am passionate about teaching. I love sharing my enthusiasm for fluid mechanics and helping students bridge the gap between complex theory and practical engineering.

Click on any of the links on top to see my latest projects!
